audit_items.proof_trace: The sole explicit free in listpack.c is lpFree (L216-218), which calls lp_free once with no subsequent use. lpInsert, the only function that calls lp_realloc, correctly saves a byte offset (poff = p-lp, L617) before any reallocation and recomputes dst = lp + poff after each realloc branch (L661, L678), ensuring the old (potentially freed) allocation is never dereferenced; the two branches are mutually exclusive so no double-realloc occurs. No other function performs allocation or free, so no use-after-free or double-free paths exist within listpack.c.
